## **Information Shared**

* RS1:  
  * Premium: Putters can be divided into (1) value (least $), (2) sub-premium, and (3) premium (most $). PG already has the SQ putter (\<$200) which is more of a value club, the RS1 would be closer to $399 most likely (premium).  
  * High Tech: Unique features offered (feature-stacking)  
  * Multi-material: steel (up front), aluminum, and composite


* Features:  
  * Potential hero-feature: “face down, face balanced” for the club to stay more square to the path  
    * Unique visual (proof)  
    * Stems in part from the multi-material (i.e., steel face)  
  * Potential hero-feature: “high forward MOI” for face twist reduction/control  
    * Stems in part from the multi-material (i.e., steel face)  
  * Shaft: potentially less torque given its thick/carbon structure  
  * Face: not the primary feature, started larger grooves and modified  
  * Visually: “Vernier acuity” with the three lines


* Other:  
  * Sound and feel go hand-in-hand  
  * With weight up front, you can *pull* it back instead of *pushing* it back  
  * Adjustable shaft for angle (stroke arc?) and right or left handed players
